mechanism_narrative: Positive monthly momentum in industrial production signals improving real activity and near-term earnings breadth; higher INDPRO growth predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest INDPRO snapshot value is 110.3034 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO monthly growth has nonpositive average 20-day forward S&P 500 returns in triggered walk-forward observations, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses upward through 2.0% and continues rising over three months, tighter discount rates reduce equity valuation multiples and predict negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.0% and rises at least 0.25pp over the prior 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest FEDFUNDS snapshot value is 2.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if upward FEDFUNDS crossing episodes produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or the triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is elevated but payrolls are expanding, markets price recovery in revenues without immediate overheating pressure; PAYEMS growth with UNRATE above 6% predicts positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is at or above 6.0% and PAYEMS increases over the prior month
evidence_refs: fact:latest UNRATE snapshot value is 6.1 and PAYEMS is 137473.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if observations with UNRATE at or above 6% and positive PAYEMS growth do not outperform baseline 60-day returns, or if conditional hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 1000 thousand units signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and future construction-linked earnings pressure, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest HOUST snapshot value is 965.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST below-1000 crossing events are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases or show no underperformance versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a federal funds rate above 1.5% signals inflation pressure met by restrictive policy, which compresses equity multiples and predicts negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises over 3 months and FEDFUNDS is above 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:latest CPIAUCSL snapshot value is 219.181 and FEDFUNDS is 2.0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-up and FEDFUNDS-above-1.5% state has average 20-day S&P 500 returns greater than or equal to unconditional average returns.

mechanism_narrative: Positive short-term growth in retail sales indicates resilient household spending and supports revenue expectations for cyclicals, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest RSAFS snapshot value is 381169.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly RSAFS growth has a 5-day forward S&P 500 hit rate below 50% or a rolling 3-year Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in unemployment signals deterioration in labor income and recession risk; when UNRATE increases materially over three months, S&P 500 forward returns are negative over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over the prior 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest UNRATE snapshot value is 6.1 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE 3-month increases above 0.3pp are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or do not underperform the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Slowing real GDP with rising CPI identifies a stagflationary demand shock in which real earnings growth weakens while discount-rate pressure persists, predicting negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth decelerates versus the prior quarter and CPIAUCSL rises over the prior 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest GDPC1 snapshot value is 11740.3 as of t-5mo and CPIAUCSL is 219.181
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1-deceleration and CPIAUCSL-increase state does not produce below-baseline 60-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward testing.
